forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over the Tasman sea, extends a ridge over southern SA. A broad trough moves over SA early next week, then a cold front clips the southeast and an upper trough moves up over SA Wednesday and Thursday. A new ridge develops south of the Bight mid next week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Possible showers mainly about the pastoral districts, possibly extending into the northern agricultural area. Possible morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures below average in the north, grading to above average in the south. Southeast to northeasterly winds, with local afternoon sea breezes.Northwest to southwesterly winds in the southeast.

Isolated showers mainly about about the pastoral and western districts, possibly extending over central districts. Possible morning fog patches about southeastern districts. Maximum temperatures below average in the north, grading to above about the agricultural areas. Southeast to northeasterly winds, with local afternoon sea breezes.

Showers about the agricultural area and south of the pastoral districts, possibly throughout the North West Pastoral district. Possible thunderstorms about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures above average, grading to below average in the northeast. Northeast to northwesterly winds, shifting west to southwesterly in the south during the day. Local afternoon sea breezes.

text.p: Showers about the agricultural area and south of the pastoral districts, possibly throughout the North West Pastoral district. Possible thunderstorms about the agricultural area and south of the Pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures above average, grading to below average in the northeast. Southeast to southwesterly winds extending throughout during the day. Northeast to northwesterly winds, shifting west to southwesterly in the south during the day, with local afternoon sea breezes.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: less than 2 mm broadly about the agricultural areas, south of the Pastoral districts and the North West Pastoral district, increasing to generally less than 5mm about the southern agricultural area, with isolated falls of 2 to 10 mm.
